original_hypothesis.title: Indefinite Burn Delay via Stale Slash Rollover
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If a SLASHER repeatedly includes a previously-slashed user in new `slash` transactions, the contract rolls over their existing `slashedAmount` to the `currentSlashRound` (even if the new slash amount is 0 due to the user having no remaining balance). This resets the 90-day `burnRoundMinimumDuration` timer for those funds, allowing a malicious or poorly-configured SLASHER to indefinitely delay the burning of slashed stakes.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This requires the semi-trusted SLASHER_ROLE to act against the protocol's intent, either maliciously or via a misconfigured off-chain bot that repeatedly flags the same bad actors. It breaks the finality of the burn mechanism.

proof_of_signal.pattern: totalSlashed[currentSlashRound] += sStake.slashedAmount; // Moves pending burn to new round

preconditions: User has a pending slashed amount from the previous round
SLASHER_ROLE includes the user in a new slash transaction

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Moving slashed amounts between rounds resets the appeal timer
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Repeated slashing prevents funds from ever reaching the lockAndBurn threshold
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Malicious slasher gives their colluding friends infinite time to appeal their slashes

economic_questions: Will the off-chain SLASHER bot deduplicate addresses that are already fully slashed?
Is there an economic incentive for a compromised SLASHER to delay burns?
validation_requirements: Verify round 1 burn amount is 0 and funds are moved to round 2

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Time value of money on slashed funds

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Prevents deflationary pressure on GTC token

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
